hero.sub: Sapiion connects everyone in the institution, while giving each role the insights and workflows they need.

cta.body: Tell us who will be in the room and we will structure the demo around what matters to you.

audiences.persona: Sandra — Programme Manager, Business Administration, 800 students, 24 teachers
audiences.reality: Sandra is responsible for educational quality, but the information she needs is fragmented. Student risks appear too late, accreditation evidence requires months of preparation, and she cannot always see whether learning outcomes are assessed consistently across the programme.
audiences.headline: Know whether your programme is delivering what it promises — before accreditation, not during it.

features.title: Programme health overview
features.body: One view of the entire programme: curriculum alignment, outcome coverage, student risk, grade distribution and attendance trends.

features.title: Continuous accreditation readiness
features.body: Every learning outcome linked. Every assessment traceable. Every decision supported by evidence.

features.title: Early warning system
features.body: Coordinators identify at-risk students earlier — giving the institution time to intervene before problems become failures.

features.title: Curriculum consistency
features.body: Teachers work from shared rubrics and learning outcomes. Programme quality no longer depends on individual interpretation.

features.title: Programme performance insights
features.body: Compare modules, cohorts and learning outcomes. Identify improvement opportunities before they become quality issues.

features.title: Workplace Learning Intelligence
features.body: Internship activity logs, company feedback and student reflections are aggregated across programmes. Identify emerging industry trends, competency gaps and curriculum areas that need updating — before they show up in declining student outcomes.

audiences.result: When accreditation comes, preparation takes days — not months. Student support becomes proactive instead of reactive. Quality improvement becomes institutional.

audiences.persona: Fatima — SLB Advisor, 45 advisees across two cohorts
audiences.reality: Student support is often reactive. By the time warning signs become visible, valuable time has already been lost. Grades, attendance and progression data are spread across multiple systems, making it difficult to build a complete picture of each student.
audiences.headline: Intervene before students fail. Not after.

features.title: Complete student profile
features.body: Attendance, assessments, learning outcome progress, interventions and risk signals in one place. No hunting across systems.

features.title: Risk signals
features.body: Prioritised risk signals help identify which students require attention now, before small issues become major problems.

features.title: Progression review workflow
features.body: A structured workflow with evidence automatically collected throughout the semester, making progression decisions transparent and defensible.

features.title: Intervention tracking
features.body: Record interventions, assign follow-up actions, schedule review moments and monitor whether support is making a difference.

audiences.result: Every student conversation starts with insight instead of administration. Progression review becomes a review process — not a collection exercise.

audiences.persona: Ahmed — Internship Coordinator, 3 programmes, 120 students on placement
audiences.reality: Coordinating internships involves countless emails, spreadsheets and phone calls. Students struggle to find placements, supervisors lack visibility into progress, and important documents are scattered across multiple systems. Monitoring dozens of internships simultaneously is time-consuming, making it difficult to identify students who need support before problems escalate.
audiences.headline: Connect students, companies and teachers through one shared workplace learning workflow.

features.title: Placement management
features.body: Guide students from application to placement with a structured workflow for CVs, motivation letters, company applications and approvals.

features.title: Live internship monitoring
features.body: Monitor activity logs, supervisor engagement and student progress in real time. Automatically identify internships that require attention.

features.title: Connected supervision
features.body: Teachers, internship coordinators, workplace supervisors and students all work within the same platform, reducing administrative overhead and improving communication.

features.title: Evidence collection
features.body: Activity logs, reflections, workplace assessments and supervisor feedback are continuously captured and linked to learning outcomes.

features.title: Workplace intelligence
features.body: Aggregate internship data across companies and cohorts to identify industry trends, competency gaps and opportunities for curriculum improvement.

audiences.result: Internships become a managed educational process instead of an administrative burden, while every placement strengthens both student success and future curriculum design.

audiences.persona: Martijn — Lecturer, HRM, 3 active classes, 78 students
audiences.reality: Martijn spends hours each week on administration instead of teaching. Rubrics are recreated for every course, and it is difficult to identify struggling students before problems become visible.
audiences.headline: Less administration. More teaching. Better insight into every student.

features.title: One connected workspace
features.body: Curriculum, lesson planning, attendance, assessment and student progress in one connected workspace.

features.title: Connected Rubrics
features.body: Create rubrics once, connect them to learning outcomes, and reuse them across modules and assessments.

features.title: Integrated Attendance
features.body: Register attendance directly from the class view — connected to the student risk signals coordinators rely on.

features.title: Connected grading
features.body: Assessment results are automatically linked to rubric criteria and learning outcomes, creating evidence of student competency rather than isolated grades.

features.title: Automatic outcome evidence
features.body: As teachers grade using rubrics, outcome coverage is recorded automatically. No separate tracking, no reporting overhead. The accreditation evidence builds itself.

audiences.result: Martijn has more time to teach and better insight into the students who need support. The connections to curriculum, outcomes and quality happen automatically — because he simply teaches inside one connected platform.

audiences.persona: Lena — Curriculum Designer, central educational development team
audiences.reality: Lena's biggest frustration is that curriculum design becomes disconnected from daily teaching. Programme documents are created, approved and stored — but she cannot see whether the intended learning outcomes are actually being delivered and assessed.
audiences.headline: Design curriculum that stays connected — from intent to evidence.

features.title: Curriculum Composer
features.body: Design programmes in a live, connected system — not static documents.

features.title: Outcome mapping
features.body: Connect modules, assignments and assessments to learning outcomes. Detect gaps before they affect programme quality.

features.title: Shared rubric library
features.body: Rubrics designed by curriculum experts flow directly into teaching and assessment. No duplication. No reinvention.

features.title: Curriculum effectiveness view
features.body: See whether your design actually produced the intended outcomes in practice.

audiences.result: Lena's curriculum design becomes a living asset. She improves programmes based on evidence — not assumptions.

audiences.reality: Amir attends most lectures but loses track of deadlines. He receives grades but does not always understand what they mean. He is somewhere in the middle — not failing, not flourishing — and often receives attention only when problems become visible.
audiences.headline: Know exactly where you stand. Before it becomes a problem.

features.title: One dashboard
features.body: All assignments, deadlines, grades and progress indicators in one place.

features.title: Outcome progress
features.body: See which competencies are developing — not just scores.

features.title: Rubric criteria before submission
features.body: He knows what "good" looks like before he submits, not after.

features.title: Early awareness
features.body: If something starts to go wrong, Amir sees it early — and knows what action to take.

audiences.result: Amir stops flying blind. He knows what to do, what is due and how he is progressing.

audiences.reality: Peter has seen too many software projects that were launched with enthusiasm and abandoned within a year. He is not against innovation. He is responsible for making sure every new platform is secure, maintainable and valuable in the long term.
audiences.headline: One connected platform that reduces complexity — not another system to manage.

features.title: One connected platform
features.body: One platform that connects curriculum, teaching and assessment while working alongside existing institutional systems. Reduce fragmentation without forcing a complete replacement of your IT landscape.

features.title: Open integration
features.body: Sapiion connects with existing institutional systems through secure APIs and standard data exchange formats — SIS, Microsoft 365, and identity providers. Designed to fit into your existing architecture, not create another isolated island.

features.title: EU hosted + GDPR
features.body: Data stored in the EU. DPA and security documentation available before implementation starts.

features.title: Full data isolation
features.body: Each institution operates in its own secure data environment. Institutional data is never mixed.

features.title: No lock-in
features.body: Full data export at any time. Always.

features.title: Controlled implementation
features.body: Start with one programme, validate adoption and workflows, then scale gradually across the institution.

audiences.result: Peter can sign the DPA with confidence. Implementation risk is controlled. Adoption is supported because every role gets clear value from day one.
